text.title: Growth Content Marketing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Growth Content Marketing methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Spec names ≥1 pillar + 3-7 cluster keywords. Without cluster structure, posts don't compound.
rule.rationale: Spec names ≥1 pillar + 3-7 cluster keywords. Without cluster structure, posts don't compound.

rule.statement: Distribution time MUST equal ≥50% of production time per piece. Publish-and-pray is the #1 failure mode.
rule.rationale: Distribution time MUST equal ≥50% of production time per piece. Publish-and-pray is the #1 failure mode.

rule.statement: Calendar covers ≥3 months ahead. Month-by-month planning loses compounding.
rule.rationale: Calendar covers ≥3 months ahead. Month-by-month planning loses compounding.

rule.statement: Spec names the founder/content lead. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.
rule.rationale: Spec names the founder/content lead. 'team' / 'we' / '' forbidden.

rule.statement: Each long-form piece atomizes into ≥5 derivative formats (X / LinkedIn / newsletter / carousel / video).
rule.rationale: Each long-form piece atomizes into ≥5 derivative formats (X / LinkedIn / newsletter / carousel / video).

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-growth-content-marketing.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
